description.en: Complete guide to washer types including flat washers, spring washers, and lock washers. Learn proper selection for construction, roofing, and industrial applications in African environments.
description.zh: 垫圈类型完整指南，包括平垫圈、弹簧垫圈和锁紧垫圈。了解非洲建筑、屋面和工业应用的正确选型。

description.pt: Guia completo de tipos de arruelas incluindo arruelas planas, arruelas de molla.

keywords: washers, flat washers, spring washers, lock washers, construction fasteners, Africa fasteners

body.en: Washers are essential components in bolted joints, serving multiple functions: load distribution across surfaces, protection of the mating material from damage, prevention of galvanic corrosion, and locking against loosening. The three main types are flat washers, spring washers, and lock washers.

rows: Maintain bolt tension, compensate for settling

heading.en: Flat Washers: The Workhorses of Construction

body.en: Flat washers (also called plain washers) are the most common washer type. They provide a smooth bearing surface that distributes the bolt load over a larger area, preventing damage to the mating material and reducing the chance of pull-through. USS flat washers have larger outer diameters while SAE washers have tighter tolerances.

Spring washers (split lock washers) feature a radial slit that creates spring tension. This tension maintains bolt preload even when vibration causes the nut to rotate slightly. In African mining applications, spring washers are essential for equipment subject to constant vibration from crushers, conveyors, and generators.

For African environments, material selection is critical. Stainless steel washers (A2/A4) offer excellent corrosion resistance for coastal applications in Ghana, Nigeria, and South Africa. Hot-dip galvanized washers are suitable for general outdoor use. Zinc-plated washers work for indoor applications.

Washers must be sized to match the bolt diameter. The washer inner diameter should slip over the bolt easily, while the outer diameter should provide adequate load distribution. For African construction projects, standard metric sizes from M6 to M24 cover most applications.
